Unfortunately, it quickly becomes disoriented when forced to fight in unfamiliar territory, preventing it from being effective outside of your ship.

name: Dual Mod Blaster Pistols
description: A pair of rare Modified Blaster Pistols. A person could burn through a lot of charger packs with these.

name: Execution Chamber
description: This is a specially designed room which can be installed in your ship. Anyone who boards you in an attempt to take over your ship will find themselves locked in this room by the self sealing doors. A pair of small portholes allow your crew to safely sweep the chamber with weapons fire from the other side of the reinforced bulkhead.

name: Guncraft
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. This particular one is starting to show small traces of rust on it, which could impact its performance and collectability. The basic model has a well balanced grip and precision sigting, but there are rumors of Guncrafts that have been transformed into terrifying weapons by skilled gunsmiths.

name: Isochronal Laser Rifle
description: The Isochronal Laser was a failed attempt by Tarazed to recreate the Skylances installed on Quarg ships. While their engineers managed to create a powerful beam, they were unable to keep it stable. 	This miniaturized rifle version has a significantly extended battery life over other laser rifles. It also runs cooler, making it unlikely to overheat from prolonged use.

name: Medical Bay
description: A medical bay staffed by a trained doctor helps keep your crew in good health, boosting morale. When defending the ship, injured crew members will likely be brought here for treatment, often making defense of the medical bay a rallying point of interest in tense boarding battles.

name: Modified Blaster Pistol
description: A complex modification allows this rare version of the Blaster Pistol to fire a more powerful energy blast, but doing so will burn out the charger pack. This has created an insatiable demand for functioning charger packs.

name: Purger Rifle
description: Purger Rifles are sometimes used as part of clandestine biohazard containment protocols. They fire Euthanasia rounds which ensure a quick and painless death for anyone unfortunate enough to be infected with experimental pathogens. Each rifle comes with four additional magazines to ensure an uninterrupted dispersal of mercy. A hollow point injector attachment holds additional inoculations for virulent infestations that enter melee range.

name: Sniper Rifle
description: A single armor piercing bullet can change the course of history. A good scope helps too.

name: Security Station (Staffed)
description: This is a specially designed barricade, which can be installed in the hallways of your ship as a powerful defense against anyone who boards you in an attempt to take over your ship. 	Dedicated security staff posted at this station helps to ensure that the ship is protected during boarding battles.

name: Tactical Laser Rifle
description: The Tactical Laser Rifle adds a spare battery pack, allowing for longer combat engagements. Its sturdier frame also requires less regular maintenance and protects the internal components if the wielder opts to use this as a bludgeoning weapon. For the additional cost, these benefits are widely considered to be negligible for non-military use.

name: Miscellaneous Cargo
description: A variety of different trade goods haphazardly stuffed into a single crate...

name: Blaster Charger
description: A charger pack for a Blaster Pistol. You might win a special prize if you bring this to Bailey in the Citadelle system and survive the Puppetmaster's challenge...

name: Contraband
description: Illegal merchandise found in a smuggler's cargo bay. You might be able to sell it for a profit if the authorities haven't caught you with it first.

name: Drone Hacking
description: "How to Hack a Science Drone For Fun and Profit." This guidebook instructs the reader on how to abuse the cargo scan fining system by hacking into a drone's scanners when boarding the drone. Contrary to the title, it works on any drone set up for scanning cargo or outfits. One important detail buried in the text is that the drone must not be hostile towards you. Keep this installed on your flagship for easy reference when you find a suitable drone to hack.

name: Eureka Map
description: A map with directions to reach a specific area on Eureka Station, an old derelict in the Ipsing system. What you'll find if you go there is unknown... 	You must have this installed on your flagship in order to use it when you land at the designated location.

name: Research Data
description: A collection of data recorded from scanning interstellar phenomena, exploring alien worlds, or investigating strange technologies. Often, these discoveries lead to more questions than answers. 	This is general data covering a wide variety of sciences but specializing in none.

name: Research Data (Bio)
description: A collection of data recorded from scanning interstellar phenomena, exploring alien worlds, or investigating strange technologies. Often, these discoveries lead to more questions than answers. 	This is biology data often associated with, but not limited to, argicultural, medical, and terraforming studies.

name: Research Data (Chem)
description: A collection of data recorded from scanning interstellar phenomena, exploring alien worlds, or investigating strange technologies. Often, these discoveries lead to more questions than answers. 	This is chemistry data often associated with, but not limited to, developing weapons and various compounds.

name: Research Data (Phys)
description: A collection of data recorded from scanning interstellar phenomena, exploring alien worlds, or investigating strange technologies. Often, these discoveries lead to more questions than answers. 	This is physics data often associated with, but not limited to, graviton, quantum, and weft theory.

name: Spirit Board
description: A simple wooden plank with letters and numbers etched onto its surface, this ancient item is associated with the occult. Supposedly, it can attract the attention of entities who exist outside our mortal realm...

name: Subsidurial Plushie
description: A stuffed toy shaped like a popular space creature. Isn't it adorable? Keep this installed on your flagship to show your support for the Subsi and Friends show!

name: Syndicate Bond (Titania)
description: This bond certifies a loan to the Syndicate for one billion credits. Ten years after being issued, bond holders can reclaim the one billion credit principal plus an additional five hundred million credits interest payment at Syndicate outfitters. 	This bond was issued to fund the creation of Titania Station in the Eteron system. Once the Syndicate has enough funding for this project, these bonds will no longer be available.

name: Syndicate Stock (Common)
description: This certificate represents ownership of one share of Syndicate common stock. Common stock grants no voting power in shareholder elections, but shareholders can still receive dividend payments. 	After the destruction of the Galactic Stock Exchange, the Syndicate began buying and selling shares of their corporate common stock through outfitters on worlds under their control.

name: Alien Torture Device
description: A machine assembled from scavenged parts that appear to be alien in origin. It creates a burst of energy that causes extreme pain. While not harmful enough to kill its victims, this device can be used to torture captured defenders until they reveal the codes for bypassing a ship's internal security locks.

Missions: Ship Sale: Modified Argosy (Screams of Madness)

name: Almachian Brain Maggots
description: A jar of writhing alien worms. After one is inserted into the victim's ear, there are several moments of agonizing pain and suffering before the victim dies. While not a practical combat weapon, the threat of being tortured to death by these creatures is often enough to persuade a captured crew member to give up the security codes for bypassing the ship's internal door locks.

Missions: Ship Sale: Modified Argosy (Screams of Insanity)

name: Balthazar Model
description: A 1/100th scale model of the District ship, Balthazar. Some assembly required.

name: Biohazard Gas
description: This illegal weapon designed for hand-to-hand combat seems to have been developed through experiments using the gas produced by Pug Biodefenses on unwilling human test subjects.

name: Carabao AI
description: This computer regulates all of the automated systems on the Carabao city ship.

name: Catapult Schematics
description: The schematics for the Asteroid Catapult that was installed on the Thermopylae space battle station. Without a skilled engineer willing to actually make one, these are just pictures on a computer screen.

name: Cloaking Device (Damaged)
description: You're sure the cloaking device is here somewhere. You can't see it, but you can feel the heat it is generating.

name: CRG-0 Android
description: While these were once considered cutting-edge outfits, their lack of autonomous capabilities and economic benefit ensured they were never licensed by a manufacturer. 	Despite this, some captains commissioned these directly from their inventor. This cargo android does little to assist with most shipboard operations. Instead, it was programmed to load cargo bays with a focus on making effective use of the available space, allowing more cargo to be crammed onto a ship. However, this can make retrieving a specific package more difficult than some would consider worth the effort.

name: Fake Coffin
description: This box appears to be a coffin. However its inner surface is lined with a material similar to interference plating. Only the most cold hearted investigator with a strong stomach would dare open it up to inspect the contents.

name: Guncraft I
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. This particular one has been kept in perfect condition. The basic model has a well balanced grip and precision sigting, but you've heard rumors of gunsmiths who possess the knowledge and skill to make parts modifications that could transform it into a terrifying weapon.

name: Guncraft II
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. The rumors of gunsmiths who can make parts modifications to improve upon this weapon are true! 	This upgraded version of the Guncraft features an improved rifled barrel, increasing the weapon's range.

name: Guncraft III
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. The rumors of gunsmiths who can make parts modifications to improve upon this weapon are true! 	This upgraded version of the Guncraft features a larger magazine for more ammo.

name: Guncraft IV
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. The rumors of gunsmiths who can make parts modifications to improve upon this weapon are true! 	This upgraded version of the Guncraft features a laser guided tactical aim assist system, displaying range data while also marking the target.

name: Guncraft V
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. The rumors of gunsmiths who can make parts modifications to improve upon this weapon are true! 	This upgraded version of the Guncraft features a magnetic rail launching system, eliminating the need for gunpowder. This requires the gun to be charged by your ship's systems regularly. 	Mag-rail Slugs aren't sold in shops, requiring you to modify regular bullets instead. This adds a small operating cost to the Guncraft to ensure you have a steady supply of ammunition.

name: Guncraft VI
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. The rumors of gunsmiths who can make parts modifications to improve upon this weapon are true! 	A magnetic rail launching system eliminates the need for gunpowder but requires the gun to be charged by your ship's systems regularly. 	This upgraded version of the Guncraft features heat resistant magazines as a safety precaution in case the ammunition explodes prematurely. 	Detonator Rounds aren't sold in shops, requiring you to modify regular bullets instead. This adds a small operating cost to the Guncraft to ensure you have a steady supply of ammunition.

name: Guncraft VII
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. The rumors of gunsmiths who can make parts modifications to improve upon this weapon are true! 	A magnetic rail launching system eliminates the need for gunpowder but requires the gun to be charged by your ship's systems regularly. 	This upgraded version of the Guncraft features an ionic resistant firing mechanism to prevent it from being affected by its own ammunition. 	Ionic Rounds aren't sold in shops, requiring you to modify regular bullets instead. This adds a small operating cost to the Guncraft to ensure you have a steady supply of ammunition.

name: Guncraft VIII
description: An old relic from the early turf wars, the Guncraft is considered by many to be the pinnacle of handgun design. The work of several elite weaponsmiths have turned it into a terrifying weapon... 	A magnetic rail launching system eliminates the need for gunpowder but requires the gun to be charged by your ship's systems regularly. 	This upgraded version of the Guncraft features radioactive shielding to protect the wielder from its ammunition. 	Atomic Bullets aren't sold in shops, requiring you to modify regular bullets instead. This adds a small operating cost to the Guncraft to ensure you have a steady supply of ammunition.

name: Leviathan Comic
description: The first issue of Drugub's comic book inspired by human ship designs. The art looks good, but you can't read Arach.

name: Leviathan Comic #1
description: The first issue of Drugub's comic book inspired by human ship designs. The art looks good, but you can't read Arach. After a decade, this has become a rare collector's item.

name: Lionheart Sponsorship
description: As part of your sponsorship agreement, Lionheart Industries will pay 500 credits per day when you use this ship. 	(Operating income will not be paid while this ship is parked.)

name: Melchior Post Card
description: An old fashioned post card with a photo depicting Melchior on the day it was launched from the Old Port on Zug.

name: Microwave Cannon
description: Papa Grudge's custom weapon. It emits a cone of blistering heat, making it difficult for the enemy to maintain a defensive position. While not immediately lethal, prolonged exposure can cause blindness, disfigurement, and ultimately leads to a slow and agonizing death. This sort of weapons modification violates several consumer safety laws and regulations, so don't expect to find one of these for sale at any legitimate outfitter.

name: Skeleton Key
description: Urban legends include tales of three "Skeleton Keys" that unlock a pirate treasure. Perhaps this is one of those legendary keys.

name: Syndicate Stock (Preferred)
description: This certificate represents ownership of one share of Syndicate preferred stock. As a preferred shareholder, you have voting power in shareholder elections. 	Shares of Syndicate preferred stock are no longer available for purchase. The last batch was issued over 200 years ago. The Syndicate still disburses dividends for existing shares owned by those fortunate enough to have them.

name: T.H.O.R.
description: Produced by Kraz Cybernetics, this prototype Tactical Heavy Offensive Robot is armed with a tactical laser rifle, a shoulder mounted gatling gun, and an arm mounted pile driver. An installable storage bay for charging, maintenance, and repairs is included. Combat testing of the artificial intelligence is incomplete and the mass production design has not yet been finalized. Therefore pre-orders are not being accepted at this time. When this is installed on a ship, a dedicated maintenance crew must be hired for it.

name: T.H.O.R. (Damaged)
description: Produced by Kraz Cybernetics, this prototype Tactical Heavy Offensive Robot has taken severe damage. The engineers on Rust in the Kraz system can probably repair it, but it won't be cheap. Although it is not able to be deployed, you should keep this installed on your ship to prevent it from being mixed in with the regular cargo.

name: Crystal Key
description: Han Sizer purportedly acquired this object from a Quarg he encountered in the Outer Rim. Supposedly it is a key that can open a path to far flung regions of the galaxy that would otherwise be inaccessible to humanity. 	In all his travels, Han never found the door. Or perhaps he did but never shared the knowledge of what lay beyond. Most scholars familiar with the tale have concluded that some, if not all, aspects of this story are completely fabricated.

name: Deep Security License
description: That's classified.

name: Inferno Dragon License
description: This secret poem is the key to access Inferno Rockets at pirate outfitters across the galaxy: 	The Inferno Dragon's claws ravage the timeless earth. 	The Inferno Dragon's presence causes all to tremble. 	The Inferno Dragon's breath boils the boundless ocean. 	The Inferno Dragon's thirst can never be quenched. 	The Inferno Dragon's wings spread across the endless sky. 	The Inferno Dragon's ambition reaches the heavens.

name: Sunracer License
description: Only pilots who've proven their skills in a timed Sunracer Qualifier can participate in Solus League races. 	Solus League races require a ship to have no crew other than the pilot and weigh less than 250 tons.

name: Solus Champion License
description: Winning first place in a Solus League race qualifies a racer to participate in Geminae League races. 	Geminae League races require a ship to have no crew other than the pilot and weigh less than 200 tons.

name: Geminae Champion License
description: Winning first place in a Geminae League race qualifies a racer to participate in Tertio League races. 	Tertio League races require a ship to have no crew other than the pilot and weigh less than 150 tons.

name: Tertio Champion License
description: Winning first place in a Tertio League race is the highest honor a Sunracer can aspire to. 	Tertio Champions are sometimes offered jobs and opportunities not available to other racing pilots.

name: Solus Token
description: Grants access to Checkpoint Solus.

name: Geminae Token
description: Grants access to Checkpoint Geminae.

name: Tertio Token
description: Grants access to Checkpoint Tertio.

name: Double Anti-Missile Turret
description: The Double Anti-Missile Turret fills the role of a medium weight anti-missile weapon. Its high rate of fire makes it an ideal choice for thinning out missile swarms.

name: Energy Cannon
description: A relic from the Alpha War, Energy Cannons fell into disuse once the war ended. Occasionally they can still be found outfitted on older ships. They are more powerful than Energy Blasters and cover a longer range. But their rate of fire is slower and the effectiveness of the projectiles diminishes the more distant the target.

name: Energy Cannon Turret
description: The turreted version of the Energy Cannon consists of a single Energy Cannon mounted onto a rotating base. If there is any one factor that lead to the decline of this weapon, it might be the relatively slow turret turn rate which made it difficult to aim at fighters that were growing increasingly nimble as newer designs were developed.

name: Isochronal Laser
description: The Isochronal Laser was a failed attempt by Tarazed to recreate the Skylances installed on Quarg ships. While their engineers managed to create a powerful beam, they were unable to keep it stable. 	These were never adopted for widespread use and Tarazed was eventually forced to stop production of them due to poor sales. Ships in the South can sometimes be found with these rare weapons installed. However the vast majority of Isochronal Lasers are still held by Tarazed itself.

name: Isochronal Laser Turret
description: The Isochronal Laser was a failed attempt by Tarazed to recreate the Skylances installed on Quarg ships. While their engineers managed to create a powerful beam, they were unable to keep it stable. 	This turreted version of the Isochronal Laser appeals to a very niche market of pilots who want an upgrade to Deep Sky's Laser Turret, but lack the space for Heavy Lasers on their ships.

name: Kludge Blaster
description: Kludge Blasters started out as a failed attempt at modifying an Energy Blaster. Despite their shady origins, the most basic version eventually gained widespread popularity as a budget alternative mining weapon and are available at many legitimate outfitters across the galaxy. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Twin Kludge Blaster
description: Pirate captains short on cash due to botched raid attempts or an unlucky night at the card tables might resort to mounting Kludge Blasters onto their ship instead of more effective weapons. Jamming two Kludge Blasters into a single gun port was one innovation that spawned copycats among other blaster designers. The localized cluster fire exhibited in them harkens back to this original design. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Triple Kludge Blaster
description: The pressures of war inspired weaponsmiths to figure out how to squeeze even more Kludge Blasters into a single gun port. This increases the damage yield at the cost of increased heat buildup. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Kludge Blaster Turret
description: Pirate captains short on cash due to smuggling jobs gone wrong, or an unlucky run at the dog races might resort to mounting Kludge Blasters onto their ship instead of more effective weapons. Unlike other blaster turrets, both barrels of the Kludge Blaster Turret fire simultaneously. It was hoped that launching the projectiles in a volley would help compensate for the weapon's slow rate of fire. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Quad Kludge Blaster Turret
description: While the Kludge Blaster is considered a poor man's weapon among pirates, tinkerers continue to see potential in connecting several of them together. All four Kludge Blasters mounted on this turret fire at the same time, launching a deadly volley of projectiles towards the target. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Hex Kludge Blaster Turret
description: Overzealous weaponsmiths crafted this arrangement of six Kludge Blasters clustered onto a single turret mount. This increases the damage yield at the cost of increased heat buildup. As with other Kludge Blaster configurations, all six fire at the same time, launching a deadly volley of projectiles towards the target. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Modified Plasma Turret
description: This Plasma Turret only has a single Plasma Cannon mounted on it. While not as fearsome as Kraz's Plasma Turret, it is far more accessible.

name: Twin Particle Cannon
description: Both barrels of the Twin Particle Cannon fire simultaneously, releasing double the force of a single Particle Cannon all at once. The size of this weapon is its main drawback, as few ships can accommodate it. While this product is fully functional, the sales department has raised concerns about its viability in the marketplace. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Particle Burst Turret
description: One of the most frequent product requests submitted to Lovelace Labs is for a turreted version of the Particle Cannon. While none have made it past the prototype testing phase before, this version looks promising. 	An issue with previous designs was jamming in the turret turning mechanism due to stress from the recoil. In addition to a reinforced turret mount, the Particle Burst Turret limits the rate of fire to a three shot burst. Lab results have been satisfactory so far, but actual combat testing is needed. 	This weapon is still in development and is unavailable to the general public. A few lucky pilots with connections within the company have been given access to prototypes for field testing.

name: Plasma Cluster Cannon
description: The Plasma Charge Cannon has been modified to take advantage of concentrated firing mounts. The power and range of each shot has increased at the expense of increasing the weapon's charge up time. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Proton Gun-CF8
description: Four out of five reviewers at Stars and Starships Magazine criticized the Proton Gun-CF8 as a blatant marketing gimmick meant to appeal to Vanguard aficionados. The lone dissenting review praised the aesthetic appeal of its projectile firing pattern. 	This weapon is set to fire in "cluster" mode, synchronizing its rate of fire when multiple copies are installed together.

name: Proton Turret-ATS
description: The Syndicate Proton Turret-ATS was designed for the cost conscious captain concerned about keeping crew wages under control. No gunner is required to operate this turret. Instead, aiming is accomplished by utilizing an advanced automated targeting system which has been in development for several years. Critics note it has lower accuracy and response times than a skilled human gunner. Despite this, some captains consider the trade off worthwhile.

name: Prototype Anti-Missile
description: This Prototype Anti-Missile requires a crewmember to manually aim and fire the turret. It was more effective than most other attempts at early anti-missile systems, which were slow to reload and rarely hit their target.

name: Silencer Beam
description: A specialized weapon used by Republic Intelligence operatives, the Silencer Beam is optimized for disabling ships instead of destroying them. This comes at the cost of increased heat accumulation.

name: Spotlight
description: This powerful light source can be installed into one of your ship's gun ports. Some miners insist that using a Spotlight to visually inspect asteroids while they work helps them improve their yield by reducing waste. 	Despite doing no damage, targeting other ships with this can still provoke them...

name: Search Light
description: A Spotlight mounted onto a rotating turret platform provides a wider area of coverage. Whether or not this can help you locate anything lurking in the darkness is uncertain. 	Despite doing no damage, targeting other ships with this can still provoke them...

name: Tri Laser Turret
description: The Tri Laser Turret was an early attempt to produce a more powerful laser turret. Deep Sky abandoned it with the development of the Heavy Laser.

name: Turbo Blaster
description: A new form of Modified Energy Blaster, the Turbo Blaster has been further modified to increase its rate of fire. The result is an incredibly powerful weapon. The heat from this blaster can accumulate quickly, especially when used in multiples. 	These weapons first appeared on pirate ships in the Dirt Belt and may hint at hidden pirate bases in the area.

name: Twin Turbo Blaster
description: The Twin Turbo Blaster's high rate of fire is capable of launching four projectiles before other twin blasters can fire their second volley. The heat generated from firing both barrels at once is considerable. 	These weapons first appeared on pirate ships in the Dirt Belt and may hint at hidden pirate bases in the area.

name: Turbo Blaster Turret
description: This is a turreted version of the Turbo Blaster, which is a Modified Energy Blaster enhanced with an increased rate of fire. These mounted blasters can cover a 360 degree arc with double the fire power of a single Turbo Blaster. 	These weapons first appeared on pirate ships in the Dirt Belt and may hint at hidden pirate bases in the area.

name: Quad Turbo Blaster Turret
description: The combined firepower of the four Turbo Blasters mounted on this turret matches more powerful weapons through sheer overwhelming numbers of projectiles. However, the internal heat generated from this weapon may be more than some ships can handle. 	These weapons first appeared on pirate ships in the Dirt Belt and may hint at hidden pirate bases in the area.

name: Quad Mod Blaster Turret
description: Four Modified Blasters mounted on a turret base provide double the firepower of a regular Modified Blaster Turret, but also double the heat generated and energy consumed.

name: Metallic Asteroid
description: The most difficult part of operating an Asteroid Catapult is locating ammunition with the right size, shape, density, and magnetic properties.

name: Asteroid Storage
description: This box of rocks holds extra asteroids for the Asteroid Catapult. Since it's basically a cargo container, it occupies your cargo hold rather than your ship's outfit systems. Three crewmembers are needed to transport the asteroids from the ship's cargo bay to the catapult and load it.

name: Asteroid Catapult
description: This is basically a large rail gun that uses exotic ammunition. Unfortunately, development of asteroid based weapons systems was banned in the Republic, Syndicate, and Free Worlds due to an unexpected development in the field of terraforming.

name: Hyperspace Nuke
description: This Nuclear Missile has been outfitted with an experimental hyperspace fuel afterburner. It can fly faster, and potentially, farther than standard Nuclear Missiles if it doesn't explode prematurely from the hull stress. This increased speed helps it outpace anti-missile systems, but it has difficulty adjusting its trajectory once fired. 	[Hyperspace nukes are a one-shot weapon: each missile occupies a gun slot, and after it is fired, the slot it was in is left empty.]

name: Inferno Rocket
description: Inferno Rockets are ammunition for the Inferno Rocket Launcher. You cannot install one unless you have first installed a launcher to fire it from. Each launcher holds up to 20 rockets.

name: Inferno Rocket Rack
description: The Inferno Rocket Rack is used to store extra ammunition for Inferno Rocket Launchers.

name: Inferno Rocket Depot
description: The Inferno Rocket Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Inferno Rockets. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Inferno Rocket Launcher
description: A weapon secretly developed through cooperation between different pirate factions, Inferno Rockets splatter their targets with a highly flammable substance.

name: Inferno Rocket Pod
description: A weapon secretly developed through cooperation between different pirate factions, Inferno Rockets splatter their targets with a highly flammable substance. 	Pods enable fighters and other light craft to carry small quantities of rockets into battle while retaining enough space for other weapons. Large ships are better off with full-size launchers.

name: Plunder Mine
description: This is ammunition for the Plunder Catapult. Typically the these consist of eight sturdy iron crates filled with pressure triggered explosives, loaded onto a pallet. 	The practice of spray painting the sides of the crates to differentiate them from normal cargo was quickly adopted when pirate captains noticed their crews would sometimes load the wrong crates into the catapult.

name: Plunder Depot
description: Pirates never developed fancy storage solutions for Plunder Mines beyond designating some cargo space for them and assigning a crew member to maintain inventory. 	If you run out of cargo space and want to carry more Plunder Mines, install a Cargo Expansion to make room for another Plunder Depot.

name: Plunder Catapult
description: Favored by smugglers, this weapon jettisons crates loaded with explosives to lure unwary pursuers into approaching a deadly trap. 	These mines can be dangerous to the ship deploying them if they detonate before they've had a chance to disperse.

name: Stinger Missile
description: A Stinger Missile serves as ammunition for the Stinger Missile Launcher. You cannot install missiles if you have not first purchased a pod, launcher, or rack to store them in. 	Mass produced under horrendous conditions on pirate worlds, Stinger Missiles utilize outdated radar tracking technology and are also easily destroyed by anti-missile weapons. Often, the slaves assigned to manufacture them are sick or elderly. In other words, the ones too weak to fight or have low potential future value. Accidental exposure to the toxic materials in these weapons during construction is common, resulting in a high mortality rate among the laborers.

name: Stinger Missile Rack
description: The Stinger Missile Rack is used to store extra ammunition for Stinger Missile Launchers.

name: Stinger Missile Depot
description: The Stinger Missile Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Stinger Missiles. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Stinger Missile Launcher
description: The use of hazardous waste as a weapon has long been a popular tactic among anarchists and terrorists. Stinger Missiles are an insidious example of this. Developed during the early turf wars, the materials to make them are readily available and there is no shortage of suppliers. 	Once it splatters the target with its dangerous payload, the residue will continue to discharge the shields or corrode the hull for some time after the initial impact.

name: Stinger Missile Pod
description: The use of hazardous waste as a weapon has long been a popular tactic among anarchists and terrorists. Stinger Missiles are an insidious example of this. Developed during the early turf wars, the materials to make them are readily available and there is no shortage of suppliers. 	Once it splatters the target with its dangerous payload, the residue will continue to discharge the shields or corrode the hull for some time after the initial impact. 	Pods enable fighters and other light craft to carry small quantities of rockets into battle while retaining enough space for other weapons. Large ships are better off with full-size launchers.

name: Swingfire Missile
description: The Swingfire Missile is ammunition for the Swingfire Missile Launcher. You cannot install missiles if you have not first purchased a pod, launcher, or rack to store them in.

name: Swingfire Missile Rack
description: The Swingfire Missile Rack is used to store extra ammunition for Swingfire Missile Launchers.

name: Swingfire Missile Depot
description: The Swingfire Missile Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Swingfire Missiles. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Swingfire Missile Launcher
description: An upgrade from Lovelace Labs popular Sidewinders, the Swingfire Missile is equipped with additional maneuvering thrusters and an enhanced targeting system. After being launched, it will recalculate a target's trajectory relative to its original position before activating its main rocket to pursue it. This increases the missile's accuracy while extending its range.

name: Swingfire Missile Pod
description: An upgrade from Lovelace Labs popular Sidewinders, the Swingfire Missile is equipped with additional maneuvering thrusters and an enhanced targeting system. After being launched, it will recalculate a target's trajectory relative to its original position before activating its main rocket to pursue it. This increases the missile's accuracy while extending its range. 	Pods enable fighters and other light craft to carry small quantities of missiles into battle while retaining enough space for other weapons. Large ships are better off with full-size launchers.

name: Thermo Detonator
description: WARNING: This device is a one-shot weapon. It will destroy your ship when detonated. A security override prevents activation unless hostiles are nearby. 	It should be noted that any evidence suggesting this outfit was installed on your ship at the time of its destruction voids any insurance policy that would have paid compensation otherwise. 	This outfit is integrated into your ship's hull and cannot be safely removed without damaging it.

name: Meteor Missile Depot
description: The Meteor Missile Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Meteor Missiles. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Twin Meteor Launcher
description: The Meteor Missile is the cheapest and simplest homing weapon available in human space, with only rudimentary infrared tracking systems that can have trouble homing in on targets with low heat levels. True to its name, the missile reaches a very high speed to leave its target's anti-missile defenses with little time to react. The trade-off is maneuverability, so Meteor Missiles are practically useless against agile targets farther than point-blank range. 	This alternative to the Meteor Missile Launcher was in common use centuries ago. With the widespread adoption of Torpedo technology, the Twin Meteor Launcher is considered an antiquated design. 	This weapon will jam if there isn't enough ammunition to fire a full volley.

name: Torpedo Storage Depot
description: The Torpedo Storage Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Torpedoes. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Twin Torpedo Pod
description: Torpedoes are the most powerful homing weapon, but they move slowly enough that fast ships can simply outrun them, making them most useful against large targets or against small ships at times when they are too distracted to dodge. 	At first glance this looks like a regular Torpedo Launcher. But a closer inspection reveals that these are a pair of Torpedo Pods jury rigged to be crammed into a single gunport. Both are programmed to activate simultaneously, launching a pair of Torpedoes when fired. 	This weapon will jam if there isn't enough ammunition to fire a full volley.

name: Ammo Basics
description: "How Not To Blow Yourself Up: A Guide To Ammunition Development." 	This guidebook gives novice weapons developers safety tips and best practices for experimenting with ammunition. A small disclaimer indicates that this is for informational purposes only. The publisher accepts no liability for any damage, injury, or death resulting from anyone reading this book. 	Keep this installed on your flagship if you want to research ammunition technologies as you fly through the galaxy.

description: You have learned all of the basic human ammunition technologies. Perhaps this foundational knowledge might prove useful in your research endeavors. 	You must still carry a copy of Ammo Basics if you want to research new ammunition technologies.

name: Builder Anti-Missile
description: The Builders anti-missile technology isn't much more advanced than what is available from Lovelace Labs. In some ways they're inferior. Their range is severely limited and they have a slow recharge time. Also, these turrets require a crew member to operate them.

name: Builder Negator
description: A rapid fire version of the Nullifier, the Negator is so powerful that it impacts the ship it is installed on with some ionic interference. If not used with discretion, it can easily drain its own ships power reserves and even cause its own internal mechanisms to jam. 	Attempts to train the Ka'het to use this weapon ended with disastrous results. They possessed no sense of restraint, constantly firing the Negator until their own energy storage systems ran dry.

name: Builder Grand Cooling
description: This powerful cooling system keeps large Builder ships from overheating.

name: Builder Grand Defense
description: Builder ships had weak shields, which necessitated development of strong hulls and hull repair technologies. Devices like the Grand Defense are a rarity, as the Builders only intended them to supplement the integrated hull repair on their largest ships.

name: Builder Grand Generator
description: The most powerful generator created by the Builders, the Grand Generator is reserved for installation on the largest of Builder ships.

name: Builder Grand Accumulator
description: A bank of power accumulators condensed to store vast amounts of energy for use on larger Builder ships.

name: Builder Hull Plating (100%)
description: Builder capital ships have extremely durable hulls. This enhanced hull plating allows a ship it is installed on to survive diving into the harsh atmosphere of a gas giant even if it wasn't specifically designed to do so. Relying on this too often will cause it to deteriorate.

name: Ka'het Cloak Armor (10%)
description: This special hull plating can enhance the cloaking abilty of Ka'het that have the ability to do so. It can also allow the Ka'het it is installed on to survive diving into the harsh atmosphere of a gas giant. However, doing so will definitely destroy it.

name: Ka'het Tactical Armor (30%)
description: Ka'het Tactical Armor is designed to facilitate evading enemy weapons fire. This can also allow the Ka'het it is installed on to survive diving into the harsh atmosphere of a gas giant. Relying on this too often will cause it to deteriorate.

description: Normally, this would give you access to restricted areas of the Builder space stations, but this particular unit is damaged.

name: Builder IFF Transmitter
description: This device is able to transmit codes which give you access to certain restricted areas of the Builder space stations.

name: Ka'het Scanner
description: A powerful scanner outfitted onto the Ka'het to gather information on hostile alien species. The Builders never envisioned it being used to gather information on themselves. 	The Ka'het scanner is incredibly sensitive, but the materials used in its construction can act as a lightning rod, making your ship more vulnerable to the effects of ionization.

description: Exile weapon design research stumbled upon an enexpected discovery when firing multiple Fire-Lances in close proximity. With just the right arrangement, it is possible to merge their beams into a single longer stream of fire that burns hotter than they would individually.

name: Inferno-Trident
description: Adding another Fire-Lance to the Blaze-Pike was a natural next step in Exile weapon design research. Mounting three them together in this array allows them to operate synergistically, creating an even longer, more powerful beam than the Blaze-Pike.

name: Scavenger Turret
description: Some Korath Exiles still use these antiquated devices in their efforts to scavenge for raw materials. An early implementation of Korath grav-ray technology, the power of the beam generated by this turret is not strong enough to move anything larger than cargo crates or other flotsam.

name: Shock-Strike Turret
description: Korath Grab-Strike projectiles are designed to pull a target closer to the Korath ship, to keep it from escaping. 	This weapon adds an ionic charge to Grab-Strike projectiles, further incapacitating the targeted ship.

name: Thermal Obliterator
description: A rare sight in the Korath arsenal, Thermal Obliterators need authorization from multiple planning committees before resources can be allocated to the construction of one. 	This weapon launches huge globs of super heated plasma that can obliterate smaller ships with a single strike. As with other thermal weapons developed by the Korath, this attack requires a lengthy cooldown period before the weapon can fire again.

name: Thermal Scorcher
description: The Thermal Scorcher is an antiquated weapon that predates the Korath civil war. It fires a large glob of super heated plasma at the target. This brutal attack generates an incredible amount of heat, requiring a significant cooldown period before the weapon can fire again.

name: Thermal Scorcher Turret
description: Only a single Thermal Scorcher is mounted on this turret. They are a rare sight now, but during the Korath civil war battleships bristling with these sorts of armaments were  common.

name: Magnetic Mine
description: This is ammunition for a Magnetic Mine Deployer. 	Development of this weapon was rushed to deployment at the request of Navarchs who have explored the Ember Wastes.

name: Magnetic Mine Rack
description: The Magnetic Mine Rack is used to store extra ammunition for a Magnetic Mine Deployer. 	These storage racks also act as a faraday cage to prevent the stored mines from accidentally interfering with the generators of the ship while carrying them.

name: Magnetic Mine Depot
description: The Magnetic Mine Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Magnetic Mines. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Magnetic Mine Deployer
description: Development of the Magnetic Mine Deployer is the result of lessons learned from the Korath's previous encounters in the Ember Waste and Graveyard. 	This new mine does less damage overall than the old Cluster Mines, but also emits electromagnetic pulses which interferes with the generators of enemy ships caught within its blast radius.

name: Piercer Missile Depot
description: The Piercer Missile Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Piercer Missiles. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Cluster Mine Depot
description: The Cluster Mine Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Cluster Mines. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Firelight Storage Depot
description: The Firelight Storage Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Firelight Missiles. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Firestorm Torpedo Depot
description: The Firestorm Torpedo Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Firestorm Torpedoes. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Microbot Corroder
description: Vindictive Korath boarding crews will attach this to the hull of an enemy ship. The Microbot Corroder creates microbots programmed to break down the hull of the ship it's installed on into raw materials which are then used to create more microbots. 	If you find this installed on your ship, get rid of it as soon as possible!

name: Botanical Garden
description: Some Korath World-Ships are outfitted with the necessary equipment to maintain artificial biomes stocked with an assortment of biological samples from their destroyed worlds. 	This Botanical Garden is a collection of trees, plants, and other vegetation that can be used for a variety of purposes including conservation, research, and food production. 	In addition to outfit space, this production outfit requires cargo space reserved for the commodities produced.

name: Korath Heat Shunt (Huge)
description: Korath weapons generate extreme amounts of heat, and the cooling systems they require can be massive.

name: Korath Scanner
description: Korath ships rarely rely on scanning technologies, preferring to shoot first and plunder later. The rare ship that does need it likely has one of these bulky systems installed. 	While not particularly powerful or sophisticated, the Korath's unorthodox approach to scanning has the unintended side effect of jamming radar based missile guidance systems.

name: Microbot Fabricator
description: Some Korath World-Ships are outfitted with the necessary equipment to operate full scale manufacturing facilities. 	This Microbot Fabricator is programmed to produce basic consumer goods such as clothing, medicine, and electronics that are easily traded at spaceports across the galaxy. 	In addition to outfit space, this production outfit requires cargo space reserved for the commodities produced.

name: Reasoning Node (Damaged)
description: This miniature computer appears to be damaged. Hopefully it wasn't an important part of the systems for the ship it was installed on.

name: Security Circuit
description: This is some sort of supplementary computer processor meant to fortify the ship's self-defense systems. Removing this from a ship it is installed on before attempting to capture it should lessen the odds of triggering its self-destruct sequence. 	Installing this onto your own ship may help prevent its computer systems from being hacked.

name: Security Circuit (Damaged)
description: This Security Circuit burned out while thwarting an aggressive hacking attempt. It can no longer protect your ship from being hacked.

name: Security Node
description: This is some sort of supplementary computer processor meant to fortify the ship's self-defense systems. Removing this from a ship it is installed on before attempting to capture it should lessen the odds of triggering its self-destruct sequence. 	Installing this onto your own ship may help prevent its computer systems from being hacked.

name: Security Node (Damaged)
description: This Security Node burned out while thwarting an aggressive hacking attempt. It can no longer protect your ship from being hacked.

name: Shield Diluter
description: Shield Diluters utilize shield disruption technology to enable other weapons to penetrate the shields of the ship they are installed on. Kor Efret boarding crews occasionally attach these to the hull of an enemy ship. 	If you find this installed on your ship, get rid of it as soon as possible!

name: Systems Core (Damaged)
description: Despite massive damage, this hybrid shield generator computer system is still operational to some degree. 	While unable to repair damage to complex systems such as itself, the machines controlled by this systems core can patch holes in the hull.

name: Systems Core (Huge)
description: This huge bank of computers, shield generators, and repair stations are designed to be used on massive World Ships and Space Stations.

name: Generator (Tinder Class)
description: Their preference for these supplemental generators instead of a standalone battery is a testament to the Korath's energy management philosophy.

name: Korath Generator (Small)
description: This miniature nuclear reactor was used by the Korath to power their fighters.

name: Korath Reactor (Large)
description: This massive reactor was designed by the Korath to meet the power needs of their largest ships.

name: Korath Thruster (Large)
description: This engine was used in the Korath capital ships.

name: Korath Steering (Large)
description: This engine was used in the Korath capital ships.

name: Fentra Predator
description: This feral creature was at the top of Sarisa Fentra's natural food chain. Like other surviving specimens kept in captivity by the Korath Exiles, the world it originated from can no longer support it. Unless kept in a proper habitat, it cannot survive in captivity. 	Fentra Predators are not trained to follow commands and offer no assistance in defending the ship. However releasing one onto an enemy vessel can be incredibly disruptive for defenders unprepared to deal with something so savage.

name: Meltdown Cannon
description: A weapon stolen from Farpoint's evidence vault, this bears a resemblance to the Korath Thermal Repeater Rifle. It launches a glob of burning plasma that can melt though titanium on contact. It's possible that this is the last one of these weapons to exist. The heat it generates is so intense that it can melt its own components if not given adequate time to cool down.

name: Seleptra Bloom
description: The Korath admire Seleptra Blooms from a distance. If a creature wanders close enough, the plant releases a cloud of spores. While not lethal to living creatures, they can induce a deep sleep that leaves its victims vulnerable to predators. If an infested creature dies, the spores inside the corpse will begin to grow. 	Spores infesting flesh processed into Nutrigel by a microbot fabricator do not survive the process, leaving a product that is safe to consume. Nutrigel made from meat infused with Seleptra Bloom spores are also more flavorful to Korath tastebuds.

name: Pug Ion Zapper
description: This Pug Zapper has been modified to add Ionic damage to the beam it generates.

name: Pug Ion Zapper Turret
description: The Pug Ion Zapper Turret delivers twice the ionic damage of the non-turreted version.

name: Pug Dual Seeker
description: The Pug Dual Seeker mounts two Pug Seekers onto a single gun port, providing increased firepower.

name: Pug Thunder Seeker
description: The Pug Thunder Seeker fires homing energy projectiles much like the regular seeker. The difference is that these are more resilient against anti-missiles and explode more violently on impact.

name: Tier 2 Anti-Missile
description: The Pug's Tier 2 anti-missile system features both more power and a longer range with no increases in energy consumption or heat generated.

name: Jump Drive (Limit Fuel)
description: The workings of the jump drive are a total mystery. The best that human scientists have been able to find out is that this device is a containment field for matter in a state that cannot normally exist in this universe; if the drive is opened up, the field collapses and its contents disappear. Therefore, even though a few jump drives have been captured over the years, it has so far proved impossible to reverse engineer them. Damage to this Jump Drive has doubled the amount of fuel it consumes with each jump.

name: Pug Prognosticator
description: This seemingly innocuous obelisk is no mere decoration. It functions as a scanner that is able to read strategic tactical data over great distances.

name: Pug Shield Supplementer
description: The Pug use this basic supplementary system to boost the already formidable shield generation on their ships.

name: Pug Shield Reinforcer
description: The Pug use this enhanced supplementary system to boost the already formidable shield generation on their ships and reinforce them against anti-shield attacks.

name: Pug Akcorlo Engines
description: These engines are intended to be the primary means of locomotion for Pug fighters and drones.

name: Pug Maretar Thruster
description: This is a huge thruster designed by the Pug. It is not quite as powerful as a similarly sized atomic engine, but it makes up for that by being almost as energy efficient as ion engines.

name: Pug Maretar Steering
description: This is a huge steering system designed by the Pug. It is not quite as powerful as a similarly sized atomic engine, but it makes up for that by being almost as energy efficient as ion engines.

name: Pug Afterburner
description: Some Pug use this afterburner to supplement their normal thrusters. Unlike regular afterburners, it consumes more energy than fuel.

name: Dream Crystal
description: The Dream Crystal is a modified Pug Peacekeeping Staff that lacks the ability to emit a beam. The Crystal still incapacitates anyone it touches by causing them to enter a euphoric and hallucinogenic state. Coming down from the state of euphoria is never pleasant. Pirates have found a variety of uses for these Crystals. Some use them to control slaves while others sell them as mind altering stimulators.

name: Pug Supplemental Shielding
description: The Pug use this basic supplementary system to boost the already formidable shield generation on their ships.

name: Elysian Trident
description: An even more destructive version of the Elysian Ranseur. While planetary defense commanders feel confident that such a display of awesome power would deter any from daring to attack the Quarg again, most tacticians do not consider the extra range and power this weapon provides to be worth the additional time spent recharging its energy coils.

name: Skyharpoon
description: These immensely powerful particle beam cannons are among the simplest weapons wielded by Quarg.

name: Void Scraper
description: This powerful ramscoop can accumulate fuel reserves even in deep space. The Quarg rarely utilize this device unless embarking on excursions far beyond the jump range of their ring worlds.

name: Jump Drive (Limit Distance)
description: The workings of the jump drive are a total mystery. The best that human scientists have been able to find out is that this device is a containment field for matter in a state that cannot normally exist in this universe; if the drive is opened up, the field collapses and its contents disappear. Therefore, even though a few jump drives have been captured over the years, it has so far proved impossible to reverse engineer them. Damage to this Jump Drive has limited its ability to travel without the aid of a hyperspace lane. Fortunately, its consumption of fuel has also been reduced by half.

name: Threat Analyzer
description: The Quarg consider their authority over the galaxy second only to the Drak. This device can be used to quickly assess suspicious ships for potential threats to the galactic order.

name: Targe Shield Generator
description: Even the smallest example of Quarg shield generation displays defensive powers greater than what humans would normally consider to be sufficient.

name: Accelerator Core
description: This antimatter device releases extreme amounts of energy in a simple, elegant package, all while producing surprisingly little waste heat.

name: Nanocell Battery
description: The energy density of Quarg batteries is unparalleled. 	Somehow, the battery chills itself as energy is drained from it, seemingly in defiance of the human understanding of thermodynamics.

name: Tiny Graviton Thruster
description: The mechanics of these tiny Quarg thrusters are inscrutable for your level of technological understanding. 	These engines also have strange capabilities which seem to violate the laws of physics as humanity understands them. Ships equipped with them accelerate faster under their own power than one would expect, while also taking less impact from external forces.

name: Tiny Graviton Steering
description: The mechanics of these tiny Quarg steering engines are inscrutable for your level of technological understanding. 	They also have strange capabilities which seem to violate the laws of physics as humanity understands them. Ships with these engines accelerate faster under their own power than one would expect, while also taking less impact from external forces.

name: Evidence of ********
description: Evidence of an atrocity that was committed. Some examples include but are not limited to: mutilated ******s, illegal *******s, and ******s of **** ***********. 	You're not sure how this got onto your ship, but it's probably in your best interest to get rid of it before you are accused of a horrible crime against all that is decent and good in the galaxy.

Missions: Midnight Lost Orphan: Captain of Atrocity
Midnight Lost Orphan: Captain of Atrocity (Capture Override)
Midnight Lost Orphan: Captain of Atrocity 2
Midnight Lost Orphan: Captain of Atrocity 3
Midnight Lost Orphan: Captain of Atrocity 4
Midnight Lost Orphan: Captain of Atrocity 5
Midnight Lost Orphan: Free Worlds Atrocity

name: Ancient Earth Pictograph
description: A picture of an artifact found in Earth ruins discovered under the western Sahara marshes. It depicts a crowd of prehistoric humans kneeling before a prominent figure of some importance. 	This photo is somewhat blurry, but still might be worth something to the right person...

name: Antimatter Core (Damaged)
description: Although this device is no longer functioning, it might still be worth something to the right people.

name: Celestial Compass
description: With the right mapping data, this alien device can project the movement of celestial objects across the galaxy.

name: Midnight's Lost Orphan
description: A parasitic incorporeal entity from another dimension. Midnight's Lost Orphan grants its host strange unnatural powers that can bend or warp time and space. 	However, the entity feeds on negative emotions and will manipulate the mental state of anyone nearby towards taking actions that cause strife, pain, and suffering among friend and foe alike. Crew productivity and teamwork takes a severe downturn whenever the entity is allowed to exert itself. A simple task that would normally take one crew member to perform can require two or three as tense arguments and disagreements hamper efficiency.

name: Nanotech Battery (Damaged)
description: Although this device is no longer functioning, it might still be worth something to the right people.

name: Obsolete Star Map
description: This star map is obsolete. Someone with the right knowledge may be able to use this data to extrapolate the current positions of these star systems.

name: Quantum Shield Generator (Damaged)
description: Although this device is no longer functioning, it might still be worth something to the right people.

name: Insurgent Subjugation Gear
description: This equipment is only used by the Quarg when it is necessary to educate violent species of the dangers resulting from insubordination. 	The Coalition are the youngest in the galaxy to have witnessed the mass deployment of these weapons. Their Enforcement Confrontation Gear is merely a weak replica when compared to the original.

name: Quarg Graviton Generator
description: A weapon that once belonged to a rogue Quarg, the Graviton Generator causes a small singularity to appear temporarily at the distance and direction set by the wielder. Anything caught within the implosion point is instantly crushed from the extreme gravitational forces generated. Unlike traditional firearms, no projectile is actually launched from the weapon, rendering line of sight seemingly meaningless. 	Due to the potential collateral damage this weapon can cause, using it to defend your own ship from boarders is ill advised.

name: Quarg Quantum Phase Pistol
description: While the rogue Quarg who previously owned this could have wielded it in one hand, humans and other smaller species need both arms to use this Quantum Phase Pistol. Once locked onto the chosen target, it creates and fires a projectile which phases through any matter that would impede its trajectory. The technology that enables this weapon to work is beyond your understanding.

name: Quarg Skytrident
description: These immensely powerful particle beam weapons strike with such intensity that some of their damage penetrates shield defenses.

name: Tiny Graviton Reverser
description: Quarg thrusters are quite powerful for their size and produce very little heat, but at the cost of consuming immense amounts of energy. 	They also have strange capabilities which seem to violate the laws of physics as humanity understands them. Ships with these engines accelerate faster under their own power than one would expect given the thrust of their engines, while also taking less impact from external forces. 	Because a reverse thruster must be facing forwards, it must be installed in the weapon section of your ship instead of the engine section.

name: Inhibitor Blaster
description: This modified version of the Inhibitor Cannon lacks the firing synchronization of the standard Remnant version. The result is that instead of firing a volley of inhibitor projectiles, a group of these weapons will produce a steady stream. Intimate knowledge of how the weapon works is necessary to make this sort of modification while leaving the weapon's other attributes unchanged.

name: Lightning Tendril
description: A weapon inspired by the electrifying Luminazoa. The tentacle is difficult to control. The gunner must often struggle to aim at a target.

name: Flare Belcher
description: A weapon inspired by the fire breathing Nova Fireflies. An extra tank of hyperspace fuel is included.

name: Quill Launcher
description: A weapon inspired by the prickly Star Gazers. There is a slight delay while it regenerates the quills used as ammunition.

name: EMP Torpedo Storage Depot
description: The EMP Torpedo Storage Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use EMP torpedoes. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Teciimach Canister Depot
description: The Teciimach Canister Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Teciimach Canisters. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: Engine Restraint
description: An Engine Restraint will reduce the mobility of any ship it is installed on. Remnant crews able to board an enemy ship may attach this device onto the enemy engine systems to sabotage their maneuverability. 	If you find this installed on your ship, get rid of it as soon as possible!

name: Heart of Nightmare
description: The still living organ of some unknown alien creature throbs and quivers aboard the Ember Nightmare. Integrated into the ship's systems, it is not clear how this is kept alive. The system data indicates that its primary purpose involves providing the ship with a crude form of stealth via radar and optical jamming. If removed through conventional means, the organ would probably die.

name: Reverberator Rifle
description: A weapon stolen from Farpoint's evidence vault, this utilizes design concepts similar to those found in the Remant Tuning Rifle. While not as refined in its ability to pinpoint organs to liquefy, it's true potential is in the echo that grows in intensity as the soundwaves reverberate down spaceship corridors, eventually becoming strong enough to knock humans off their feet. As the sounds bounce off walls, they can even hit targets hiding behind corners. Unfortunately, this weapon is useless outside of the confines of a spaceship.

name: Remnant Fabricator License
description: The underlying secrets of Remnant technology are a closely guarded secret known only to those with the responsibility to produce it. Compartmentalization is key to safeguarding critical information.

name: EMP Torpedo Access Temporary License
description: This technical manual details how to manufacture EMP Torpedos. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer EMP Torpedos even if it is normally not stocked there. 	The underlying secrets of Remnant technology are a closely guarded secret known only to those with the responsibility to produce it. Compartmentalization is key to safeguarding critical information. 	This tech can be researched instead of purchased. (Hover/Tap the attributes below for more details.) 	After this is acquired it can be found in the Licenses area in future visits to the outfitter.

name: EMP Torpedo Tech License
description: This technical manual details how to manufacture EMP Torpedos. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer EMP Torpedos even if it is normally not stocked there.

name: Teciimach Canister Access Temporary License
description: This technical manual details how to manufacture Teciimach Canisters. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer Teciimach Canisters even if it is normally not stocked there. 	The underlying secrets of Remnant technology are a closely guarded secret known only to those with the responsibility to produce it. Compartmentalization is key to safeguarding critical information. 	This tech can be researched instead of purchased. (Hover/Tap the attributes below for more details.) 	After this is acquired it can be found in the Licenses area in future visits to the outfitter.

name: Teciimach Canister Tech License
description: This technical manual details how to manufacture Teciimach Canisters. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer Teciimach Canisters even if it is normally not stocked there.

name: Fake Sheragi Anti-Missile
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Sheragi Anti-Missile on the Emerald Sword.

name: Fake Sheragi Cannon
description: This is a convincing recreation of a Sheragi Particle Waveform Cannon.

name: Fake Sheragi Cyclotron
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Heavy Ion Cyclotron from the Black Diamond.

name: Fake Sheragi Fabricator
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Shard Fabricator on the Black Diamond.

name: Fake Sheragi Turret
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Particle Waveform Turret on the Emerald Sword.

name: Fake Sheragi Cooling
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Large Hybrid Cooling on the Emerald Sword.

name: Fake Sheragi EWS
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Electronic Warfare System on the Emerald Sword.

name: Medium Hybrid Cooling
description: This Sheragi cooling system works both actively and passively to dissipate heat while including a cryogenic fuel tank which is used as a temporary heat reservoir.

name: Fake Sheragi Battery
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Large EM Battery on the Emerald Sword.

name: Medium EM Battery
description: This superconductive coil stores power while providing a boost to the shield regeneration of the ship.

name: Fake Sheragi Drive
description: This is a convincing recreation of the Fusion Drive on the Emerald Sword.

name: Reactor Drive
description: A smaller version of the Fusion Drive, the Reactor Drive still includes a hyperdrive, a reactor core, and powerful plasma engines.

name: High-Velocity Spike Access Temporary License
description: This technical manual details how to manufacture Star High-Velocity Spikes. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer High-Velocity Spikes even if it is normally not stocked there. 	This is technically not meant to be for sale. "But seeing as how thou art well-esteemed amongst the High Houses, perchance our outfitter shall be willing to impart it unto thee, shouldst thou be inclined to furnish us with an exorbitant sum, no less than ten times its rightful worth..." 	This tech can be researched instead of purchased. (Hover/Tap the attributes below for more details.) 	After this is acquired it can be found in the Licenses area in future visits to the outfitter.

name: High-Velocity Spike Tech License
description: This technical manual details how to manufacture High-Velocity Spikes. Having this makes it possible for any outfitter to offer High-Velocity Spikes even if it is normally not stocked there.

name: Old House Secrets
description: The data collected from scanning the mission target. The Old Houses will execute you if you are caught in posession of this.

name: New House Secrets
description: The data collected from scanning the mission target. The New Houses will execute you if you are caught in posession of this.

name: Shadow House License
description: House Sado, a relic from an antediluvian age, still operates in the shadows of a once great civilization. As their willing pawn, you can receive certain services and missions from them.

name: "Myiara" Fire Spout
description: Denounced as a "knave's weapon", this repurposed Inductive Reverser is designed to spew out superheated gouts of flaming hyperspace fuel. A "Myiara" Reverser Interlink can connect two "Myiara" Fire Spouts to synchronize their functionality as an engine.

name: High-Velocity Spike Depot
description: The High-Velocity Spike Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use High-Velocity Spikes. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to maintain inventory.

name: "Ej-Byiaa" Shield Pallium
description: Predecessor shield generators are designed to function independently, making them incompatible with a Cortex Interface.

name: Multimode Armor: Adaptor
description: The metamaterial armor of Successor warships can be altered in an appropriately equipped spaceport to change its attributes. 	This configuration allows modern Successor warships to implement Predecessor multimode configurations. It also adds some rudimentary hull repair, even to ships that don't already have it.

name: Multimode Armor: Cloak
description: You're sure the multimode cloaking armor is here somewhere, but you can't see it. 	Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration that allows the armor to cloak if the ship's hull is undamaged.

name: Multimode Armor: Blood
description: Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration that allows a vessel to partially shrug off effects that would push or pull it off course. This a mere side effect of its true purpose, which allows a vessel equipped with it to safely pass through unstable wormholes.

name: Multimode Armor: Graviton
description: Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration that allows a vessel to partially shrug off effects that would push or pull it off course. 	There is some speculation that this configuration has some deeper function that has since been long forgotten...

name: Multimode Armor: Thermal
description: Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration that insulates the armor against heat and is highly resistant to burning.

name: Multimode Armor: Pristine
description: Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration that strengthens the armor's structure against corrosion.

name: Multimode Armor: Regen
description: Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration that boosts hull repair, even adding it to ships that don't already have it.

name: Multimode Armor: Stealth
description: Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration that causes the armor to interfere with certain hostile scanning and tracking methods.

name: Multimode Armor: Plate
description: Implementing the Predecessor's multimode configurations onto modern Successor warships requires a few extra steps and materials that are unknown to most... 	This material results in a configuration of layered plates that appears to be an early attempt at multimodal hardened armor.

name: "Tundra" Espionage Scanner
description: The Predecessors strategic scanners were used to discern the unfamiliar technologies of other species. They could also track data that their Successors cannot.

name: "Irja" Fusion Core
description: This appears to be a Predecessor reactor. It is far superior to the "Nnesa" Fusion Core used by the High Houses. Their larger reactors must have been similarly impressive.

name: "Myiara" Reverser Interlink
description: This device is required to synchronize engine functionality to a pair of "Myiara" Fire Spouts.

name: Photon Blade
description: An inelegant weapon from a less civilized age, this is an artifact from the age of the Predecessors. Many knights of the old empire lost at least one of their tentacles while engaging in Photon Blade duels. 	Given enough time, this weapon can probably cut through stone. Avoid striking shiny surfaces like the Successor's liquid metal armor. The reflection of the blade could prove fatal.

name: Thunderhead Pod
description: A primitive version of the modern Thunderhead Launcher. The Thunderhead Pod is almost entirely dependent on external ammunition storage, resulting in a slower rate of fire. Unlike the modern launchers, these pods do not fire simultaneously when multiples are installed on a ship.

name: Thunderhead Storage Depot
description: The Thunderhead Storage Depot stages extra ammunition in your cargo bay for weapons that use Thunderhead Missiles. The ammunition is ready to be withdrawn for use. Storing it this way uses more cargo space than if it were packed away in sealed crates. An extra crewmember is required to oversee maintenance of the ammunition.

name: Anti-Ionic Plating
description: The result of Wanderer research into the nature of ionic damage, resistance, and protection.

name: Armament Pacifier
description: When faced with the threat of being boarded, Wanderer crews may place this device onto the attacking ship. Using an Armament Pacifier is unlikely to help the defending crew. However, any ship this is installed on will have trouble using its weapons to attack other ships. 	If you find this installed on your ship, get rid of it as soon as possible!

name: Wanderer Advanced Cooling
